description: Commutative unital rings: definitions, ring homomorphisms, ideals, prime and maximal ideals, quotient rings, generation of ideals
Integral domains: definition, zero divisors, fraction fields, subrings of fields
Noetherian rings: definition (ACC on ideals / every ideal is finitely generated), basic properties
Principal ideal domains (PIDs): definition and basic properties, every PID is a UFD
Unique factorization domains (UFDs): definition, examples, relationship to PIDs
Local rings: definition (unique maximal ideal), basic properties
Krull dimension of a ring: supremum of lengths of chains of prime ideals, dim A <= 1 condition
Integral elements over a ring, integral closure, integrally closed domains
Modules over commutative rings: definitions, module homomorphisms, finitely generated modules, submodules
Tensor products of modules, base change (M tensor_A B)
Finite separable field extensions, algebraic extensions
Polynomial rings k[x], k[x,y] over fields; k(x) as fraction field of k[x]
Gaussian integers Z[i] and subrings thereof (Z + 2iZ)
Finite fields F_p and F_q, reduction modulo p
Discrete valuation rings (DVRs): definition, uniformizers, valuation on fraction field, residue fields. From Lecture 1.
Theorem 1.16 (Lecture 1): An integrally closed noetherian local domain of dimension 1 is a DVR
Number fields and their rings of integers, as defined in Lecture 1
Global fields (number fields and global function fields are the two types), as discussed in Lecture 1
Properties of contraction and extension of ideals under localization: b^{ce} = b for B = S^{-1}A. References [1, Prop. 11.19] and [2, Prop. 3.11].
Proof of Theorem 2.1 (prime ideal correspondence under localization). The lecture cites [1, Cor. 11.20] and [2, Prop. 3.11.iv] rather than giving a proof.
S^{-1}M is isomorphic to M tensor_A S^{-1}A. Reference [2, Prop. 3.5].
Z[sqrt(-13)] is not a UFD: (1+sqrt(-13))(1-sqrt(-13)) = 2 * 7 = 14 gives two distinct factorizations. Stated without proof as a well-known fact.
k[x,y] is a UFD but not a Dedekind domain (for any field k). Stated without proof.
Every DVR is integrally closed. Used without proof in Proposition 2.9.
Invertible fractional ideals form an abelian group under multiplication (associativity, commutativity, identity, inverses). Stated as immediate from definitions.
